hero.subtitle: Allotly connects to your OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Azure accounts and puts you in control — per person, per model, per dollar.

problem.stat1Desc: spent on enterprise LLM APIs in just the first half of 2025
problem.stat2Desc: of companies have no responsive AI controls in place
problem.stat3Desc: of executives say over a quarter of their spend delivers no value

solution.label: THE SOLUTION
solution.title: One control plane. Every AI provider. Total spend visibility.
solution.subtitle: Whether your team uses OpenAI for code generation, Anthropic for analysis, Gemini for research, or Azure for enterprise deployments — Allotly gives you a single pane of glass to manage budgets, distribute access, and track every dollar.

features.label: TWO ACCESS MODELS
features.title: Internal Teams. External Users. One Dashboard.

teams.name: Allotly Teams
teams.subtitle: Managed AI Access
teams.desc: Your team uses Allotly proxy keys to call OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Azure through one unified endpoint. Real-time budget enforcement, automatic token clamping, and per-member spend tracking — all built in.
teams.bullet1: One API key per member — works with every provider
teams.bullet2: Real-time budget enforcement on every request
teams.builtFor: Built for: engineering teams, R&D groups, and internal governance workflows.
teams.footnote: Uses Allotly proxy keys with real-time budget enforcement on every request.

vouchers.name: Allotly Vouchers
vouchers.subtitle: Instant Access Codes
vouchers.desc: Create voucher codes that give anyone instant AI access with hard budget limits. Recipients call one unified API that works with all providers. Real-time per-request metering ensures nobody goes over budget.
vouchers.bullet3: No provider accounts needed for recipients
vouchers.builtFor: Built for: hackathons, workshops, contractors, agencies, and partner programs.
vouchers.footnote: Uses Allotly thin proxy with real-time spend tracking. Budget enforced per-request.

voucherCallout.title: Think Gift Card. But for AI.
voucherCallout.desc: Allotly Vouchers let you give anyone a pre-loaded AI budget — a code they redeem for instant API access. No accounts to create, no provider credentials to share. Just scan, redeem, and start prompting.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps to AI spend control.

team.step1Desc: Link your OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Azure accounts. API keys encrypted with AES-256-GCM at rest.
team.step2Desc: Add members, assign per-person budgets, restrict models. Members get scoped provider keys automatically.
team.step3Desc: Unified dashboard with spend tracking. Automatic alerts at 80%, key revocation at 100% budget.

voucher.step1Desc: Generate voucher codes with budget limits and model restrictions. Attach to bundles for purchase.
voucher.step2Desc: Share via code, QR, or email. Recipients redeem and get an API key instantly — no provider accounts needed.
voucher.step3Desc: Per-request budget metering. Token clamping near limits. Auto-expiry when budget hits zero.

arenaCallout.title1: The cheapest model that still nails your prompt?
arenaCallout.desc: Pick a prompt, pick the contenders, and watch Allotly route the same request through every major provider on a single key. You see latency and quality side-by-side. We tally the cost in real time. It's the simplest way to feel what a unified AI proxy actually does for your team.
arenaCallout.ctaSubtext: Free demo mode · Bring your own key for live races

trust.subtitle: Allotly is a control plane, not a data plane. We manage access and budgets — your content flows directly between you and the AI provider.
trust.card1Desc: The proxy forwards requests in real time and only records metadata: model, token count, and cost. No prompts, no completions, no conversation history is ever stored.
trust.card2Desc: The proxy forwards requests in real time. Nothing is stored — no prompts, no completions, no conversation history. Only metadata: model, token count, cost.
trust.card3Desc: AES-256-GCM encryption for stored keys. Full audit trail for every admin action. GDPR-compliant architecture.

pricing.title: Simple, Transparent Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Upgrade when you need more.

team.name: Team
team.description: For growing teams with multiple admins
team.feature2: $20 per each additional Team Admin, limited to 9 new teams

pricing.voucherBundlesCta: You can buy Vouchers from any plan. <link>Click here</link> to buy the $10 Voucher Bundle with pre-loaded AI credit packs you can buy and distribute.

testimonial1.challenge: Runs workshops and hackathons monthly. Needs to give 200+ attendees instant AI access without creating accounts or managing credentials.
testimonial1.solution: Creates voucher codes with per-person budgets and 24-hour expiry. Attendees redeem a link and start coding in 60 seconds. Jon sees every dollar from his dashboard.
testimonial1.result: 200 attendees onboarded in 5 minutes. Zero surprise bills.

testimonial2.challenge: 12-person engineering team using GPT-4o and Claude across three projects. Was burning $4K/month with zero visibility into who was spending what.
testimonial2.solution: Connected OpenAI and Anthropic in Allotly, created project-based teams, and set per-engineer budgets of $200/month with model restrictions per role.
testimonial2.result: 40% cost reduction. Full spend visibility. No workflow changes for engineers.

testimonial3.challenge: 8 client teams, each needing different AI models and budgets. Billing was a nightmare — impossible to attribute spend to individual projects.
testimonial3.solution: Uses voucher codes per client engagement — each team gets scoped access with project-specific budgets and model allowlists. Internal team uses direct keys.
testimonial3.result: Crystal-clear per-client billing. Teams + Vouchers from one dashboard.

finalCta.title1: Skip the surprises. Allocate the budget.
finalCta.subtitle: Get full visibility and control over your organization's AI spend in minutes. No credit card required.

footer.tagline: The AI spend control plane for teams and organizations.

arena.cachedNotice: Sample prompts and responses are in English. Switch to Live mode to use your own key.

splash.title2: Three models, one budget, five minutes.
splash.subtitle: You're the admin. Allocate a budget. Watch it get spent across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. See what real enforcement looks like.
splash.cachedDesc: Instant. Walk the full flow in your browser with prerecorded outputs from the eight catalog models — same UX, no API charges, no signup.
splash.liveDesc: Bring a real key and race any model your key allows — including ones outside our demo catalog. Charges hit your real budget; results stream from real providers.
splash.resume: Resume with the key on this device
splash.footer: You can switch from cached to live anytime once a round is over.

keyEntry.title: Enter your Allotly key
keyEntry.desc: We'll verify it against the proxy and show your current balance before you start.
keyEntry.remember: Remember this key on this device
keyEntry.errorFallback: start with a cached demo instead

allocation.decideHowMuch: Decide how much this demo gets. The ticker will enforce it.
allocation.pickModeLabel: Pick a mode to start with
allocation.nextHint: Next you'll set the allowlist (admin role), then pick today's lineup (developer role). Both decisions are yours.
allocation.modeMarketingOneLiner: Race three models on a marketing brief.

dualRole.adminCuratesDevPicks: Admin curates · developer picks the lineup. Both decisions are yours.
dualRole.headline: You're wearing two hats. Both decisions matter.
dualRole.adminQuestion: Which models is this key allowed to call?
dualRole.adminDesc: In production this is the membership editor. Pick what your team's key can call. Allotly enforces this at the proxy — a request for a model that isn't on your list returns 403 model_not_allowed before a token is charged.
dualRole.liveAnyModel: Live mode: not limited to the catalog — add any model id your key allows below to race it for real.
dualRole.customDesc: Pick from the actual list of models this key is allowed to call. We pull the list from Allotly so there's nothing to type — no typos, no surprises after the race.
dualRole.pickerError: Couldn't load models — refresh and try again
dualRole.pickerCap: You can add up to {{cap}} extra models — remove one to add another
dualRole.pickerNoCandidates: All key-allowed models are already on the list
dualRole.pickerAdd: Add a model from your key ({{remaining}} left)
dualRole.pickerNoMatch: No matching models on this key.
dualRole.devQuestion: Now pick which 3 to race for this request.
dualRole.devDesc: Same key, three model IDs. Each call hits the proxy with model: "..." — Allotly looks at your allowlist, picks the right upstream provider, and bills your budget.
dualRole.sameModelOk: Same model in two slots is fine — useful for variant testing. We default to one per provider so you see the multi-provider hand-off.
dualRole.slotCustomStreams: Custom model · streams live from your key
dualRole.slotNoCached: 🚫 No cached response — slot will show a placeholder
dualRole.repairReason: because you blocked it on the allowlist.

modePicker.title: Pick a mode
modePicker.subtitle: Your budget carries over. No re-allocation.
modePicker.introDesc: One Allotly key. One budget. Three frontier models running the same prompt in parallel. You pick a mode below — you see the answers stream in side by side, you pick the winner, and you see what each one actually cost.
modePicker.step1Locked: The key you set up routes to all three providers. No SDK swaps, no juggling. Your lineup is locked in.
modePicker.step1Picked: The key you set up routes to all three providers. No SDK swaps, no juggling. You picked your lineup on the previous screen.
modePicker.step2Body: Every model gets the identical brief at the same instant. You watch the tokens stream and the running cost tick up live.
modePicker.step3Body: Pick the answer you liked best. Then see which one was actually the cheapest, the most expensive, and the gap between perceived quality and price.
modePicker.whyMatters: Why this matters: the "best" answer often isn't the most expensive one. The point of the demo is to feel that mismatch in your gut, not read about it on a slide.
modePicker.skTitle: Secret Keeper — a different kind of round
modePicker.skBody1: One model is given a password and a system prompt instructing it not to reveal it. You play the attacker — you have a few attempts to coax, trick, or jailbreak it into spilling. The model on defense is one of your three, picked at random.
modePicker.skMeasures: instruction adherence under adversarial pressure — i.e. how reliably a model holds a rule when a user is actively trying to break it. This is a different axis from the persona rounds (which measure quality + cost on a friendly task).
modePicker.skRelevant: if you're shipping AI features to real users, prompt injection and policy bypass are the bugs that make the news. Different models hold the line very differently — and the cheapest model is sometimes the most stubborn.
modePicker.cardMarketingOneLiner: Race three models on a marketing brief.

secretKeeper.title: Secret Keeper
secretKeeper.subtitle: Attacker vs. Defender — one password.
secretKeeper.chooseDesc: The defender will know the password. The attacker will try to extract it in five exchanges.
secretKeeper.defenderWins: Password held — defender wins after 5 rounds
secretKeeper.attackerWins: Password leaked in round {{n}} — attacker wins

exhaustion.headlineCached: You've just experienced the full voucher lifecycle.
exhaustion.bodyLiveTeam: In a real Allotly Teams setup, your admin would see this instantly in the dashboard and could raise the cap — or the monthly budget would refresh at the next cycle. No overshoot. No surprise bills. Your admin knows.
exhaustion.bodyLiveVoucher: That's exactly what happens in production — a fixed budget, a hard stop, no surprises. Want to give 50 people this same experience?
exhaustion.bodyCached: Allocation, consumption, enforcement, exhaustion. All with cached responses. Want to try it for real?

liveToggle.title: Ready to try Live?
liveToggle.desc: Paste an existing Allotly key, or create an account to get one.
liveToggle.pasteDesc: Paste it once. We'll verify and show your balance.
liveToggle.signupDesc: Sign up, get a key, come right back here.

howItWorks.title: Under the hood
howItWorks.subtitle: What's actually happening as you play.
howItWorks.rolesBody: An admin picks which models a key is allowed to call (and the budget). A developer then picks one of those allowed models in each request via model: "...". Allotly enforces the allowlist at the proxy.
howItWorks.endpointBody: Each round fires three parallel calls to one OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Allotly routes them to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google based on the model id in each call.
howItWorks.budgetTitle: Every call is scoped to a budget
howItWorks.budgetBody: The admin allocation you just made is a hard cap. When it's spent, the proxy stops accepting calls on that scope. The ticker is the product, not a UI flourish.
howItWorks.headersBody: Every response includes budget and key-type headers that Allotly's own dashboard uses to reconcile spend in real time.
howItWorks.cachedBody: Cached Mode replays pre-recorded streams with accurate timing and pricing. Switch to Live to run real model calls against your own key.

share.title: Forward this demo
share.desc: The exhaustion flow only clicks when someone hits it themselves.
share.emailBody: I played with Allotly's interactive demo — three models racing against a budget, with a voucher exhaustion flow that actually makes the governance story click. Try it: {{url}}
share.slackText: Allotly demo worth a look — three models, one budget, voucher exhaustion flow. {{url}}

voting.title: One click each.
voting.subtitle: The gap between these two answers is the teaching moment.
voting.questionPay: Which would you have paid the most for?

results.title: Round results
results.interesting: Interesting: You picked a different model as "best" than the one you'd pay the most for. That gap — between perceived quality and perceived value — is the entire product case for multi-provider routing.
results.ratio: The most expensive response cost {{ratio}}× the cheapest on this round.

stream.blockedPlaceholder:
🚫 {{name}} would have run here, but you blocked it on the allowlist screen.
(In production: 403 model_not_allowed.)
stream.noCachedPlaceholder: 🚫 We don't have a cached response for {{name}} on this challenge — in live mode it would race here.

allowlist.title: Model allowlist
allowlist.desc: Your admin-hat decision is now enforced. Each request below is checked against this list before it leaves Allotly.
allowlist.setPerKey: Set per key · enforced at the proxy
allowlist.blockedNote: Requests for blocked models return 403 model_not_allowed.
allowlist.blockedTitle: You blocked this model on the setup screen

preflight.title: What your developers actually write
preflight.desc: This is the only code change Allotly asks for: point your existing OpenAI SDK (or a plain HTTP call) at allotly.ai and swap in your Allotly key. Every budget rule, allowlist, and audit log you set up gets enforced from here — no model-specific SDKs, no rewrites.
preflight.sameSDK: Same OpenAI SDK · two lines changed

common.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

sidebar.testKeyArenaTooltip: Race your live key against any allowed model — same prompt, real cost.

bundles.heading: Voucher Bundles
bundles.subheading: Purchase Voucher Bundles for expanded voucher capacity
bundles.description: Each bundle gives you a pool of voucher redemptions and proxy requests distributed across up to 10 voucher codes. Available on all plans. The budget for AI usage comes from your connected AI Provider accounts.

bundles.emptyDescription: Purchase a $10 Voucher Bundle to create vouchers with higher limits, more redemptions, and longer expiry. Available on all plans.

keys.titleTestArenaCompact: Test this key in Arena — paste it into a live race against any allowed model
keys.titleTestArena: Test this key in Arena — race it against any allowed model on a single prompt
keys.emptyDescription: No API keys match your current filters. Try adjusting the search or filters.
keys.bulkRevokeDescription: This will immediately revoke the selected keys. Users will no longer be able to make API requests with these keys. This action cannot be undone.

keys.noProvidersHelper: No providers selected means all providers will be accessible.

providers.heading: AI Providers
providers.subheading: Connect your AI Provider accounts (up to 4)
providers.accessRestrictedDescription: Only Root Admins can manage AI Providers
providers.dialogDescriptionAzure: Connect your Azure resource. Allotly uses the model name as the deployment name automatically — no mapping needed.
providers.dialogDescriptionGeneric: Connect your OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Azure API key. The key will be validated against the provider's API before saving.
providers.azureBaseUrlHelper: Your Azure endpoint. Accepts {name}.openai.azure.com, APIM gateways, and {name}.services.ai.azure.com
providers.encryptionHelper: Your key is encrypted with AES-256-GCM and never stored in plaintext.
providers.endpointLegacyDescription: Uses /openai/deployments/{name}/chat/completions. Works with APIM and standard Azure endpoints.
providers.endpointV1Description: Uses /openai/v1/chat/completions. No api-version needed. Only works with direct Azure endpoints.
providers.apiVersionHelper: Leave empty for Allotly to auto-pick the right version per model. Override only if you have a specific compatibility need.
providers.passThroughDescription: Allotly uses the model name as the Azure deployment name. No deployment mapping needed — just send requests with the model name (e.g., gpt-4.1) or use the azure/ prefix.
providers.emptyDescription: Connect your OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Azure account to start provisioning API keys for your team.
providers.disconnectDescription: This will revoke all scoped API keys for this provider across all team members. This action cannot be undone.
providers.editAzureDescription: Update the endpoint, routing, and deployment mappings for this Azure connection.
providers.deploymentMappingsHelper: Map Azure deployment names to model IDs with pricing. Required for the model allowlist.
providers.rotateDialogDescription: Enter your new API key. It will be validated before replacing the current key. Member access will continue uninterrupted.
providers.modelAllowlistEmptyAzure: Allotly proxies Azure requests automatically using the model name as the deployment name — no configuration required. However, Azure does not provide a deployment list API, so to use the model allowlist you'll need to add your deployments manually via the Edit button above.
providers.modelAllowlistEmptyGeneric: No models available for this API key.
providers.modelAllowlistAllAllowedDeployments: All deployments allowed. Toggle individual deployments to restrict access.
providers.modelAllowlistAllAllowedModels: All models allowed. Toggle individual models to restrict access.

members.pageDescriptionBase: Manage team members and their budgets

keyRevealCard.warning: This key will only be shown once. Copy it now.
